headline: LRT3 Shah Alam Line Opens 18 Months Behind Schedule
context: LRT3 Shah Alam Line commenced partial operations in January 2026 eighteen months behind original June 2024 completion target.

cards.big: LRT3 Shah Alam Line opens 18 months late with only 15 of 26 stations
cards.sub: Remaining 11 stations postponed to 2027 due to construction and testing delays

cards.big: Project cost reached RM16.6 billion up from initial RM9 billion in 2015
cards.sub: Cost escalation of 84% makes it most over-budget rail project in Malaysian history

cards.big: Daily ridership in first month averaged only 45,000 against projected 120,000
cards.sub: Incomplete stations mean most commuters cannot reach destinations without transfers

cards.big: Prasarana reported RM280 million in interest costs during 18-month delay
cards.sub: Finance Ministry reviewing whether contractor penalties cover the overruns

cards.big: Partial opening tests whether incomplete infrastructure delivers promised public benefit
18 months late, LRT3 still opened. The question is whether penalties match the cost of delay.
